open_questions: PDF text extract failed for all 505 indexed files (full=0).** Re-run OCR on English IR before next refresh.
Confirm FY2026 actuals and FY2027 guide when released.

Reconcile thesis_card why_market_wrong prose still referencing legacy ¥4,490 quote vs Yahoo ¥2,591 (2026-07-21).
Component schedule uses inventory-tier English IR citations until extract succeeds.
thesis: DataSection Inc. (3905.T) is a Japanese technology company pivoting from legacy IT services into GPU-backed AI data centers under its Neo Cloud and TAIZA brands. **[Fact]** Customers are cloud and enterprise tenants; revenue comes from colocation, compute rental, hosting, and add-on services tied to installed GPU capacity. **[Fact]** FY2026 guidance (English IR, inventory tier) calls for **¥33,601M** revenue versus **¥2,942M** in FY2025, with parent net profit **¥2,804M** and guided EPS **¥115.57**. **[Fact]** May 7, 2026 disclosure secured **5,080 B300 GPUs** via Compal (~**USD 325M**), with Inzai site operations phased from May to July 2026. Financing mixes customer advances (**¥13.6B**), borrowings (**¥30.6B** planned), and a 23rd stock-acquisition-rights issue (**¥6.7B**).
why_market_wrong: The market may be over-penalizing a **transitory problem**: Giga B200 overhang (**577** units, **USD 252M** unpaid) and financing-timing fear ahead of energization, while underweighting a binding hyperscaler-linked ramp if Inzai starts on schedule. **[Inference]** At **¥2,591** (Yahoo 2026-07-21), the stock trades below proof-first component base **¥2,953/sh**, so part of the discount may reflect execution fear rather than fully priced success. **[Inference]** Conversely, inventory-tier guidance may still embed optimistic utilization; PDF extract failure keeps filing verification open.
